It is the sixth site of the {{ill|lt=Izumo Province Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism|Q11395860|WD=Q11395860|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deity is {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsumiko / Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}, with {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime / Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto and Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} enshrined together as associate deities. === History === It appears in the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}' as “Mefu no Yashiro” (賣布社), and in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' as “Mefu Jinja” (賣布神社). It is said that the shrine was originally located on the western shore of the Irie of Ōi ({{ill|lt=Lake Shinji|Q1151248|WD=Q1151248|1=Lake Shinji}}). Due to tidal currents and changes in topography, it was relocated eastward multiple times, and around the {{ill|lt=13th century|Q7049|WD=Q7049|1=13th century}} it came to be enshrined at its present location. There was also a period when it was known as Shirakata Daimyōjin or Hashihime Daimyōjin. === Subsidiary shrines within the precincts === * Wadatatsumi Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Funadama Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Dōsojin Shrine * Shirakata Tojin Arakami * Jōkō Shrine * Ōmatsu Arakami === Festivals === * January 8: Shiokai-sai * July 12: Funamiyuki Shinto rite * October 9: Takibi Shinto rite * October 10: {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}, Gokyō-sai (Suzuki Festival) === Surroundings === Located in the urban area of Matsue City. The {{ill|lt=Ōhashi River|Q11436587|WD=Q11436587|1=Ōhashi River}} flows to the north of the shrine, and the Matsue Shin-Ōhashi Bridge connects it to the northern bank. === Access === * Approximately 500 meters northwest from {{ill|lt=Matsue Station|Q1058652|WD=Q1058652|1=Matsue Station}} on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Mefu Shrine}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:出雲国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]]

It is the sixth site of the {{ill|lt=Izumo Province Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism|Q11395860|WD=Q11395860|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deity is {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsumiko / Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}, with {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime / Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto and Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} enshrined together as associate deities. === History === It appears in the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}' as “Mefu no Yashiro” (賣布社), and in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' as “Mefu Jinja” (賣布神社). It is said that the shrine was originally located on the western shore of the Irie of Ōi ({{ill|lt=Lake Shinji|Q1151248|WD=Q1151248|1=Lake Shinji}}). Due to tidal currents and changes in topography, it was relocated eastward multiple times, and around the {{ill|lt=13th century|Q7049|WD=Q7049|1=13th century}} it came to be enshrined at its present location. There was also a period when it was known as Shirakata Daimyōjin or Hashihime Daimyōjin. === Subsidiary shrines within the precincts === * Wadatatsumi Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Funadama Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Dōsojin Shrine * Shirakata Tojin Arakami * Jōkō Shrine * Ōmatsu Arakami === Festivals === * January 8: Shiokai-sai * July 12: Funamiyuki Shinto rite * October 9: Takibi Shinto rite * October 10: {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}, Gokyō-sai (Suzuki Festival) === Surroundings === Located in the urban area of Matsue City. The {{ill|lt=Ōhashi River|Q11436587|WD=Q11436587|1=Ōhashi River}} flows to the north of the shrine, and the Matsue Shin-Ōhashi Bridge connects it to the northern bank. === Access === * Approximately 500 meters northwest from {{ill|lt=Matsue Station|Q1058652|WD=Q1058652|1=Matsue Station}} on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:島根県の旧県社]]

It is the sixth site of the {{ill|lt=Izumo Province Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism|Q11395860|WD=Q11395860|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deity is {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsumiko / Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}, with {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime / Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto and Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} enshrined together as associate deities. === History === It appears in the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}' as “Mefu no Yashiro” (賣布社), and in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' as “Mefu Jinja” (賣布神社). It is said that the shrine was originally located on the western shore of the Irie of Ōi ({{ill|lt=Lake Shinji|Q1151248|WD=Q1151248|1=Lake Shinji}}). Due to tidal currents and changes in topography, it was relocated eastward multiple times, and around the {{ill|lt=13th century|Q7049|WD=Q7049|1=13th century}} it came to be enshrined at its present location. There was also a period when it was known as Shirakata Daimyōjin or Hashihime Daimyōjin. === Subsidiary shrines within the precincts === * Wadatatsumi Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Funadama Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Dōsojin Shrine * Shirakata Tojin Arakami * Jōkō Shrine * Ōmatsu Arakami === Festivals === * January 8: Shiokai-sai * July 12: Funamiyuki Shinto rite * October 9: Takibi Shinto rite * October 10: {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}, Gokyō-sai (Suzuki Festival) === Surroundings === Located in the urban area of Matsue City. The {{ill|lt=Ōhashi River|Q11436587|WD=Q11436587|1=Ōhashi River}} flows to the north of the shrine, and the Matsue Shin-Ōhashi Bridge connects it to the northern bank. === Access === * Approximately 500 meters northwest from {{ill|lt=Matsue Station|Q1058652|WD=Q1058652|1=Matsue Station}} on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

It is the sixth site of the {{ill|lt=Izumo Province Sacred Sites of Shinto and Buddhism|Q11395860|WD=Q11395860|1=Izumo Shinbutsu Pilgrimage}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal deity is {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsumiko / Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayakitsuhime-no-Kami|1=Hayakitsuhiko and Hayakitsuhime}}, with {{ill|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Kami|Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Ooyatsuhime / Tsumatsuhime|Q10935047|WD=Q10935047|lt=Ooyatsuhime-no-Mikoto and Tsumatsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Oyatsuhime and Tsumatsuhime}} enshrined together as associate deities. === History === It appears in the '{{ill|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|Q11395858|WD=Q11395858|1=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki}}' as “Mefu no Yashiro” (賣布社), and in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' as “Mefu Jinja” (賣布神社). It is said that the shrine was originally located on the western shore of the Irie of Ōi ({{ill|lt=Lake Shinji|Q1151248|WD=Q1151248|1=Lake Shinji}}). Due to tidal currents and changes in topography, it was relocated eastward multiple times, and around the {{ill|lt=13th century|Q7049|WD=Q7049|1=13th century}} it came to be enshrined at its present location. There was also a period when it was known as Shirakata Daimyōjin or Hashihime Daimyōjin. === Subsidiary shrines within the precincts === * Wadatatsumi Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Funadama Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Dōsojin Shrine * Shirakata Tojin Arakami * Jōkō Shrine * Ōmatsu Arakami === Festivals === * January 8: Shiokai-sai * July 12: Funamiyuki Shinto rite * October 9: Takibi Shinto rite * October 10: {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}, Gokyō-sai (Suzuki Festival) === Surroundings === Located in the urban area of Matsue City. The {{ill|lt=Ōhashi River|Q11436587|WD=Q11436587|1=Ōhashi River}} flows to the north of the shrine, and the Matsue Shin-Ōhashi Bridge connects it to the northern bank. === Access === * Approximately 500 meters northwest from {{ill|lt=Matsue Station|Q1058652|WD=Q1058652|1=Matsue Station}} on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]

It is said that the shrine was originally located on the western shore of the Irie of Ōi ({{ill|lt=Lake Shinji|Q1151248|qid=Q1151248|1=Lake Shinji}}). Due to tidal currents and changes in topography, it was relocated eastward multiple times, and around the {{ill|lt=13th century|Q7049|qid=Q7049|1=13th century}} it came to be enshrined at its present location. There was also a period when it was known as Shirakata Daimyōjin or Hashihime Daimyōjin. === Subsidiary shrines within the precincts === * Wadatatsumi Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Funadama Shrine * Ebisu Shrine * Dōsojin Shrine * Shirakata Tojin Arakami * Jōkō Shrine * Ōmatsu Arakami === Festivals === * January 8: Shiokai-sai * July 12: Funamiyuki Shinto rite * October 9: Takibi Shinto rite * October 10: {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}}, Gokyō-sai (Suzuki Festival) === Surroundings === Located in the urban area of Matsue City. The {{ill|lt=Ōhashi River|Q11436587|qid=Q11436587|1=Ōhashi River}} flows to the north of the shrine, and the Matsue Shin-Ōhashi Bridge connects it to the northern bank. === Access === * Approximately 500 meters northwest from {{ill|lt=Matsue Station|Q1058652|qid=Q1058652|1=Matsue Station}} on {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West). === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mefu Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:松江市の建築物]] [[Category:松江市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shimane Prefecture]]
